title: `package.json` omits the public `./harness` export and its packed files
reasoning: The test suite treats `@gitlawb/openclaude/harness` as a supported public entrypoint, but the real package manifest only exports `./package.json`, `./dist/cli.mjs`, and `./sdk`. The `files` whitelist also excludes `dist/harness.mjs` and `src/entrypoints/harness.ts`, so a published tarball cannot satisfy the harness import contract even though the consumer test expects it to work.
recommendation: Add a real `./harness` export in `package.json` and include the harness runtime/type files in `files`, or remove the harness consumer assertions if that entrypoint is not intended to ship.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The synthetic consumer test fabricates its own package manifest, so by itself it would not catch a tarball missing the harness entrypoint. The separate exports-resolution test does catch the mismatch, but it currently describes the failure rather than preventing it.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an npm-pack-style test that installs the produced tarball into a temp project and compiles `import { registerAgentHarness } from '@gitlawb/openclaude/harness'` against the real packed artifact.
